title: FAQ
intro: A nested content example built to feel normal on the frontend while still exercising richer structure in Tentman.
body:
## Why this page is here

The FAQ is the clearest small example of nested collections in a site that still feels believable. It keeps the frontend plain while making the data model richer than a basic singleton.

You can also use :doc-link[the About page]{href="/about" variant="plain"} or :doc-link[the blog posts]{href="/blog" variant="strong"} to cross-reference other parts of the fixture directly from markdown.

sections.title: Content model
sections.intro: The FAQ page is a singleton, but its sections and questions are nested repeatable blocks.

questions.question: Why use an FAQ instead of a generic docs section?
questions.answer: Because it stays small. This fixture only needs one nested example that feels real and gives Tentman enough structure to exercise.

questions.question: What exactly is nested here?
questions.answer: Each **section** contains a repeatable list of **questions**, and each question still gets a markdown answer field.

sections.title: Frontend rendering
sections.intro: The site does not over-design the FAQ. The structure itself is the point.

questions.question: How should this look?
questions.answer: Simple headers, short intros, and quiet dividers. If the content model breaks, the page should make that obvious.

questions.question: Does the FAQ need animation or accordion chrome?
questions.answer: No. It can, but the fixture is more useful when it stays plain and easy to inspect.
